'''Greater Galam''' was a [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Lan system]], the fifth from the [[Star/Legends|star]] [[Lan (star)|Lan]], within the [[Hook Nebula/Legends|Hook Nebula]], in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. As a gas giant, the planet lacked a solid surface and was therefore, incapable of supporting life. It was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by sixteen [[Moon/Legends|moons]]. Greater Galam was not [[Ore/Legends|ore]]-rich, unlike the rest of the [[Star system/Legends|system]]. It was due to the system's richness in those resources that led to its discovery by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] sometime around [[1300 BBY/Legends|1300 BBY]], bringing many [[Mining/Legends|miners]] and colonists into the region. The [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] natives of the world [[Lan Barell/Legends|Lan Barell]], the [[Qieg/Legends|Qieg]], eventually formed an alliance with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] colonists, forming a governing body, the [[Human-Qieg Guild]], which administrated over the entire system. In due [[Time/Legends|time]], the Lan system came under the control of the powerful [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], although the latter lacked any official presence in it.<ref name="LB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Greater Galam was mentioned in the [[1994]] article "[[Lan Barell (article)|Lan Barell]]," written by [[Paul Sudlow]] and later published within [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 1|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 1]].
